meta.project_name: VOID Tattoo Fest 2026 - Interactive Booking Map
meta.contact_note: This JSON is the current technical handoff for this repo. It replaces older references to jupitermapv1.svg, jupiter.png, and jupitervoid.json with the v2 file names used by the checked-in app.

purpose.high_level: Provide an embeddable, interactive map of the Jupiter Hotel campus for VOID Tattoo Fest 2026. The map shows booth and room categories, supports list-style browsing by artist/vendor/sleep-room type, and is designed to show live availability/fullness once the Apps Script occupancy feed is re-enabled.
primary_users: Festival organizers monitoring booth and room inventory
Tattoo artists and vendors choosing a booth or room
Attendees or participants booking vendor spaces or overnight rooms

current_repo_state.summary: This repo is a retargeted v2 static map. The frontend has been updated to use the new VOID Fest assets, but live occupancy and booking-link behavior are not fully wired from the current JSON yet.
current_repo_state.asset_folder_note: The top-level assets/ directory contains the standalone SVG icon assets used by the map and its key.

high_level_architecture.intended_live_flow: Bookwhen confirmation emails in Gmail Inbox -> Google Apps Script parser -> connected Google Sheet as the publishing gate -> Apps Script Web App JSON feed -> static frontend on GitHub Pages.
high_level_architecture.current_frontend_mode: index.html currently has FEED_URL set to an empty string, so occupancy fetches return an empty object and the app behaves as a static/offline map.

components.name: Bookwhen
components.role: Source of truth for purchasable bookings. Each booking generates a confirmation email.

components.name: Gmail label pipeline
components.role: New Bookwhen emails should remain in Inbox until parsed. Successfully parsed threads should be archived out of Inbox and labeled 2026-admin---bookwhen-parsed. The old 2026-admin-bookwhen---confirmations label was not useful and should not be required for the new flow.

components.name: Google Apps Script parser
components.role: Parses Bookwhen Inbox threads, writes inventory/space_state/booking_events/parser_errors tabs, and exposes a public JSON feed through doGet().

sheets.name: booking_events
sheets.purpose: Proposed audit log table. One row per meaningful Bookwhen parser event. This is for traceability, not direct manual editing.

sheets.name: space_state
sheets.purpose: Proposed primary publishing table for the map. One row per active/current space record, editable by the user. Routine parser runs must not overwrite manual edits.

sheets.name: sleep_state
sheets.purpose: Sleep-room-only export/check-in tab for sharing with the venue. Contains lodging rows only and is separate from the public map publishing tab.

sheets.name: artist check in
sheets.purpose: Registration/check-in operating list. Deferred until after the map publishing sheet/parser workflow is designed.

components.name: Apps Script Web App
components.role: Public HTTP endpoint that returns a privacy-safe JSON summary of occupancy. The frontend is prepared to fetch this, but FEED_URL is disabled in the current checked-in index.html.

components.name: Front-end
components.role: Single static page with inline CSS and JavaScript.
components.visual_rendering_note: The public page loads voidfest-map.svg without using CSS filter inversion. Map-layer fill colors in CSS, JS, SVG, and voidfest-map-data.json are normalized to the working palette. Overlay labels and SVG icon overlays remain direct white/black styling. The sidebar header uses a CSS-masked purple swirl asset at assets/swirlpurple-header.png. Booking Options and selected booking results panels can use optimized background photos with dark/purple overlays. The mobile WTF panel supports floor-specific full-color photo grids and short venue descriptions; Jupiter NEXT 2F, Jupiter Original 1F, and Jupiter Original 2F are populated.

parser_redesign_notes.status: Initial Apps Script build pushed with clasp to the Sheet-bound script 1ujLmhmNPQeOduuv5aqIVwAQafDFbyczdX4qri9GdeMpE4pmoebmDsvzl. Parser targets connected Sheet 1OC0oAJZQf5kjQInaxbq_WxPCDl6IXDUNmKP9FbDojX8.
parser_redesign_notes.why_change: The previous bookings_raw-driven approach contained too much private/raw email data for public-map use while still not providing enough normalized operational structure for occupancy, cancellations, ticket changes, and manual map publishing control.
parser_redesign_notes.current_priority: Design the parser and connected Sheet as the gate that publishes occupancy to index.html. Attendee/check-in automation is explicitly deferred.

gmail_queue_model.unparsed: Bookwhen emails stay in Gmail Inbox. No separate confirmations label is required for unparsed mail.
gmail_queue_model.parsed_behavior: After a message is confidently parsed, apply the parsed label and remove the INBOX label so it is archived under the parsed label.
gmail_queue_model.failed_parse_behavior: Do not move failed/uncertain messages to parsed. Leave in Inbox and optionally add a future needs-review label.

booking_events.purpose: Normalized audit/event table. This replaces using bookings_raw directly as live occupancy data.
booking_events.row_grain: One row per meaningful ticket/space event extracted from a Bookwhen email.

space_state.purpose: Primary map publishing table. This should drive the public feed instead of raw email rows.
space_state.manual_edit_rule: Manual edits in this table must be preserved. A routine parser run should only create new rows for new Bookwhen emails and should not overwrite existing user-edited values.
space_state.bookwhen_change_rule: A Bookwhen cancellation or ticket-changed email is allowed to update affected rows because it represents an explicit upstream change event.

parser_errors.purpose: Diagnostics for Bookwhen emails that matched the Inbox query but could not be confidently parsed.

artist_check_in.purpose: Registration/check-in list for artists and possibly associated team members. Should be automatically populated from booking_events where possible.
artist_check_in.known_context: User previously filled this as artists booked. Desired new behavior is to automate it as much as possible from parsed booking emails.
artist_check_in.target_export_in_repo: parser script info/2026 Attendee List - Artist Check In.csv

implemented_local_files.email_parser_script.gs: Inbox-driven parser, Sheet setup, audit logging, no-overwrite space_state creation, Bookwhen change/cancel release handling, and doGet() feed from space_state.
implemented_local_files.inventory_data.gs: Generated embedded inventory data from parser script info/parser_inventory_from_map.csv for Apps Script seeding/lookup.
implemented_local_files.appsscript.json: Local Apps Script manifest using V8 and Gmail/Sheets scopes.
implemented_local_files..claspignore: Restricts future clasp pushes to Apps Script files and manifest.

clasp_project.push_status: Pushed appsscript.json, email_parser_script.gs, and inventory_data.gs with clasp.

implemented_sheet_tabs.inventory: Seeded by seedInventorySheet(); source lookup for artist and sleep spaces only. Vendor spaces are intentionally excluded because vendors do not book through this parser workflow.
implemented_sheet_tabs.space_state: Map publishing gate. New active Bookwhen events create rows if missing. Routine parser runs do not overwrite existing rows.
implemented_sheet_tabs.sleep_state: Venue-facing sleep-room export. New active sleep events create rows if missing; cancellation/change-from can mark matching rows cancelled. Venue notes are left untouched.
implemented_sheet_tabs.booking_events: Append-only audit log keyed by event_id.
implemented_sheet_tabs.parser_errors: Diagnostics for messages that could not be confidently parsed.
implemented_sheet_tabs.sheet_drift: Read-only drift report generated by checkSheetDrift(). It reports likely inconsistencies without auto-fixing anything.

implemented_parser_behavior: Searches Gmail Inbox for Bookwhen messages instead of requiring the old confirmations label.
On successful parse, applies 2026-admin---bookwhen-parsed and archives the thread out of Inbox.
Leaves failed/uncertain parses in Inbox and records parser_errors.
Excludes vendor rows from generated parser inventory.
Handles sample new booking, booking cancelled, and ticket changed emails from parser script info.
For ticket changed samples, releases From-space rows, creates To-space rows when missing, and keeps additional active sleep-room rows.

important_examples.new_booking: Dream Tent 1 10x10 with Buy-out (Full Booth), public team/shop name, website/Instagram, and multiple other artists in booking details.
important_examples.booking_cancelled: Cancellation email for an existing room/booth booking; should release or deactivate the corresponding current occupancy while preserving audit history.
important_examples.ticket_changed: One Bookwhen ref can include a cancelled/transferred-from ticket, one or more active/transferred-to tickets, and possibly a sleep room under the same ref. Parser must output multiple normalized events rather than one flat row.

registration_automation_goal.summary: Use parsed booking emails to build the artist check-in list automatically.
source_fields: Other artists included in your booking
Ticket status, cancellation, transfer, and quantity
fields_that_parser_can_likely_fill: Artist Email when present in Bookwhen fields or can be collected later
IG when present in Bookwhen fields
Sleeper when a lodging booking can be associated with the artist or booking ref
Notes for related refs, parser uncertainty, or cancellation/transfer markers
open_design_questions: Whether every artist named under 'Other artists included in your booking' should become a separate check-in row.
Whether purchaser/lead contact should always become an attendee row or only when they are also an artist.
How to mark cancelled/transferred artists in check-in history versus current active check-in list.
Whether sleep-room-only purchasers should appear in artist check-in or a separate lodging/admin list.
Which check-in columns from the existing Google Sheet must be preserved exactly.
How to preserve manual registration edits when the parser refreshes generated rows.
registration_automation_goal.important_modeling_note: The attendee/check-in sheet is person-oriented. The occupancy feed is space-oriented. They should share booking refs and space keys, but should not be the same table.
registration_automation_goal.status: Deferred. Current design focus is the map publishing sheet and parser behavior.

semantics.space_type: Parser/feed type, e.g. STD10TENT, MDL, LDL, SLEEP.
semantics.parser_scope: FALSE for vendor/VOID rows that the Bookwhen parser should ignore.

semantics.purpose: Human-editable publishing gate for the interactive map feed.
semantics.shape: Intentionally compact. Detailed parser metadata lives in booking_events and inventory.
semantics.no_overwrite: Routine parser runs create missing active rows only. They do not overwrite existing rows.
semantics.change_exception: Bookwhen cancellation/ticket-changed emails may update affected rows to release/cancel them.

semantics.purpose: Venue-facing lodging/check-in export. This tab is sleep-room only.
semantics.privacy: This contains lodging lead contact details for venue operations, so share intentionally and separately from the public map feed.
semantics.no_overwrite: Routine parser runs create missing active sleep rows only. They do not overwrite venue_notes.

semantics.event_id: Deduplication key built from Gmail message id, ref, event type, and space key.

semantics.purpose: Messages that matched the Inbox query but could not be confidently parsed. These stay in Inbox.
Simple read-only consistency report. Generated by checkSheetDrift(); does not mutate operational tabs.
checks: space_type mismatch between space_state and inventory
duplicate active rows for a space_key
sleep_state rooms not found in inventory or not typed SLEEP
active sleep room mismatches between space_state and sleep_state

space_key_normalization.hotel_rooms: 3-digit room subjects normalize to the same 3-digit key, e.g. '250'.
space_key_normalization.arium_flex: A-series booths normalize to lowercase keys, e.g. 'A5' -> 'a5'.
space_key_normalization.dream_tent_artist: Dream Tent numbered booths normalize to plain numbers, e.g. 'DT5' -> '5'.
space_key_normalization.dream_tent_vendor: Dream Tent vendor letters normalize to lowercase letters, e.g. 'DTV-G' -> 'g'.
space_key_normalization.jupiter_original_vendor: Original outdoor vendor letters normalize to lowercase letters, e.g. 'VO-H' -> 'h'.
space_key_normalization.jupiter_next_vendor: NEXT indoor vendor letters normalize to lowercase double letters, e.g. 'VN-AA' -> 'aa'.
space_key_normalization.street_fair: Street fair keys are supported by the parser as 'sf#', but README says street fair spaces are out of scope for this v2 map.

space_type_derivation.purpose: The connected Sheet should include a space_type column so the map feed can group and color rows without relying only on frontend hard-coding.
space_type_derivation.rule: After deriving space_key, classify it against known inventory/list membership. The user described this as: if the key is a Dream Tent key, space_type is Dream Tent; if it is in the MDL list, space_type is MDL; if it is in the sleeper list, space_type is SLEEP; and similarly for the other booth/room/vendor types.
space_type_derivation.recommended_source: Use void-space-inventory.csv / generated inventory data as the authority for mapping space_key -> booth_type/space_type, with parser fallback regexes only for safety.
space_type_derivation.vendor_scope: Vendor spaces do not book through this parser workflow and should be excluded from the parser inventory sheet. Vendor rows may still exist in frontend map data for display, but they are not part of Bookwhen email parsing or the connected parser Sheet.

examples.dream_tent_artist_keys_1_to_6: STD10TENT or Dream Tent artist, depending on final naming preference
examples.vendor_letter_keys: VND10TENT, VND10OUT, or VND10IN based on inventory/building

booth_types.STD10IN: Standard indoor 10x10 artist booth in Arium/Flex
booth_types.VND10STREET: Street fair vendor space, supported in legacy code but out of scope for current v2 data
booth_types.SLEEP: Frontend logical type for overnight hotel rooms; the source JSON often keeps their original room booth_type and overrides them in index.html

capacity_by_type.note: The server and frontend disagree on SDL default capacity. The frontend usually prefers csv.capacity_1_to_4 when present, but the mismatch should be reconciled before live occupancy is trusted.

ticket_rules.meaning: Ticket name patterns determine slot usage and whether a booking is treated as a full buyout.

sleep_privacy.rule: Sleep rooms must expose only status in the public feed. No names, socials, lead names, or groups should be emitted for sleeper keys.
sleep_privacy.risk: If bookings for 239, 244, or 249 are parsed while the script still lacks those keys, public feed output may expose non-sleep occupant fields for rooms that the frontend/data treat as sleep rooms.

<space_key>.status: '' or 'full'; sleepers must rely on this only
<space_key>.name: Primary public name for the first group, non-sleeper only
<space_key>.social: Primary public social/URL for the first group, non-sleeper only

groups.name: Public-facing team/shop/artist name
groups.ticket: Ticket label used for slot calculation

do_get_feed.frontend_status: index.html has the fetch code and refresh hooks, but FEED_URL is empty in the current repo.

frontend.hosting: Static site hosted on GitHub Pages; README lists the public demo as https://mrsannaclarke.github.io/book_void_2027/.

index_html.role: Single-page app with all CSS and JavaScript inline.

key_resources.FEED_URL: Currently empty string; set to a deployed Apps Script Web App URL to enable live occupancy.

panels: Mobile floor switcher and mobile map key
layout.mobile_floor_switcher: At the mobile/quadrant breakpoint, only NEXT 2F, OG 1F, and OG 2F are exposed as floor buttons. NEXT 1F remains visible only as part of the full desktop map.
layout.embedding: The page is designed to work as an embeddable static page and as a direct GitHub Pages page.

manual_debug.window.getOccupancy: Fetches the configured occupants feed; currently returns {} because FEED_URL is blank.
manual_debug.window.refreshNotes: Refetches occupants, reapplies map colors, and rerenders the last selected list.

voidfest_map_svg.role: Current vector overlay geometry and labels. It carries the working category fill colors directly and does not depend on CSS filter inversion. Numeric room IDs often appear as underscored SVG IDs, e.g. _202, and are normalized by the frontend.

voidfest_map_png.role: Raster base/reference image rendered from the current map artwork.

voidfest_map_data_json.role: Static metadata for each map shape. Includes working_color_palette/color_map and maps shape keys to { bookwhen_url, csv, svg }. csv.booth_type, csv.capacity_1_to_4, csv.building_label, csv.Color Code, and csv[Published Status] are important.
voidfest_map_data_json.current_gap: All top-level bookwhen_url values are empty in this JSON as reviewed. The inventory CSV contains Bookwhen URLs, but the frontend does not currently read the CSV.

void_space_inventory_csv.role: Working inventory scaffold with Bookwhen URLs, display labels, capacity, color code, and publication status.

void_space_review_json.role: Small mismatch/review summary. Current review_key_count is 1 for key 'ij', a combined vendor spot.

shape_lookup.notes: The frontend resolves multiple naming conventions including numeric rooms, A-series booths, letter vendor spaces, and Dream Tent numeric keys.

dream_tent_join_status.status: The old DT-join-key issue appears implemented in index.html.
dream_tent_join_status.details: deriveSpaceKeyForRow converts dt5-style candidates to '5', and candidateFeedKeys_ tries both numeric and dt/d-prefixed variants.

full_gradients.non_sleep: When full and published, non-sleep shapes use the category color fading to #ffffff and click through to the matching legend/list category.
full_gradients.sleep_rooms: Available sleep rooms are solid #6878e8; occupied sleep rooms use a #6878e8 to #ffffff gradient and are not clickable.

preserve_color.effect: Shapes in PRESERVE_COLOR keep their SVG fill and are not recolored by the live occupancy overlay.

click_behavior.available_shapes: If a shape is published and has a bookwhen_url, decorateShape() marks it .shape-interactive and wireLink() opens the booking URL in a new tab.
click_behavior.current_booking_link_gap: Because voidfest-map-data.json has empty bookwhen_url values, normal booking clicks are currently inert even though void-space-inventory.csv contains Bookwhen URLs.
click_behavior.full_shapes_non_sleep: When live feed marks a published non-sleep shape full and the booth_type maps to a legend category, clicking jumps to that category list instead of opening Bookwhen.
click_behavior.full_sleep_shapes: When live feed marks a sleep room full, the shape should be disabled and the list should show Occupied without names/socials.

legend_and_lists.legend_filters: LEGEND_FILTERS in index.html maps categories to booth_types and grouping rules.

categories.streetfair: Legacy category for VND10STREET; current README says street fair spaces are out of scope for this map project.
categories.vendor: VND10TENT, VND10OUT, VND10IN grouped by Dream Tent / Jupiter Original / Jupiter NEXT.
categories.ariumflex: STD10IN A-series booths grouped into Arium vs Flex Lounge.
categories.small_deluxe: SDL / SSDL small deluxe artist rooms, grouped by accessible vs stair-only floors.
categories.medium_deluxe: MDL rooms, grouped by accessible vs stair-only floors.
categories.large_deluxe: LDL / XLDL rooms, grouped by accessible vs stair-only floors.
categories.sleep: SLEEP rooms grouped into Opener/Entourage/Headliner/Encore. Occupant names/socials must never be displayed.

publication_control.current_code_behavior: isPublishedKey_ only checks top-level mapData.published or mapData.unpublished arrays. If neither exists, every shape is treated as published.
publication_control.current_data_behavior: voidfest-map-data.json does not have top-level published/unpublished arrays. It stores per-row csv['Published Status'] values instead.
publication_control.risk: No Listing and Unpublished rows are treated as published by current code. Some may be non-clickable only because their Bookwhen URLs are empty, but they can still appear in lists/coloring unless filtered elsewhere.
publication_control.recommended_fix: Either generate top-level published/unpublished arrays in voidfest-map-data.json or update isPublishedKey_ to read csv['Published Status'] for each shape.

color_source_of_truth.index_html: CSS and JS constants use the working_color_palette values for map fills, labels/icons, map key strokes, and full gradients to white.
color_source_of_truth.voidfest_map_svg: The active map SVG carries the working category fill colors directly on map shapes where possible.
color_source_of_truth.voidfest_map_data_json: working_color_palette and color_map use the same values; each shape csv Color Code is normalized to a working palette category.

data_consistency_and_mapping.space_key_definition: space_key is the canonical identifier used to join map shapes, JSON rows, inventory rows, and booking-feed rows.

source_of_truth_notes.voidfest_map_data_json: Frontend source for shapes/categories; currently lacks Bookwhen URLs.
source_of_truth_notes.void_space_inventory_csv: Working source that currently contains Bookwhen URLs and publication status.
source_of_truth_notes.email_parser_script_gs: Runtime source for parsing confirmation emails and publishing occupancy.
source_of_truth_notes.parser_script_info_VOID_Map_Occupants_bo: Example export of last year's occupancy-driving raw table. It is space/ref oriented and has 20 columns.
source_of_truth_notes.parser_script_info_2026_Attendee_List_Ar: Example target check-in export. It is person oriented and has 22 columns.

known_mismatches: Brief v1 asset names were stale; current app uses voidfest-map.svg and voidfest-map-data.json.
CSV has Bookwhen URLs; JSON does not.
CSV per-row Published Status is not honored by isPublishedKey_ unless converted into top-level arrays or read directly.
Apps Script sleeper keys are missing 239, 244, and 249 compared with frontend/data expectations.
Server and frontend SDL default capacities differ.

known_behaviors_and_expectations.sleeper_privacy: Sleep rooms should never display names or socials publicly. Map/list occupancy must be derived from status only.
known_behaviors_and_expectations.auto_refresh: When FEED_URL is configured, index.html periodically calls refreshOccupantsAndRerender() every 60000 ms.

coupon_code.current_copy: At checkout, enter GETAROOM to take $100 off any overnight sleeping room. One use per purchaser; cannot be combined with other offers.
coupon_code.location: Hard-coded in the Notes panel area of index.html.

known_behaviors_and_expectations.local_preview: README says index.html can be opened directly or served via python3 -m http.server 8000. A local server is safer for fetch behavior.

prerequisites: Comfort with vanilla JavaScript and DOM manipulation; no frontend framework is used.
Basic understanding of Google Apps Script, Gmail labels, Google Sheets, and Apps Script Web App deployment.
Ability to update static JSON/SVG data and verify behavior in a browser.
local_setup_steps: Open this repo at /Users/Anna/Documents/Void/Maps/VOID Fest Interactive Map v2.
Run python3 -m http.server 8000 from the repo root, then open http://localhost:8000.
Use index.html as the main entry point; it fetches voidfest-map.svg and voidfest-map-data.json.
For production occupancy testing, deploy the Apps Script Web App and set FEED_URL in index.html.
For offline occupancy testing, temporarily use a local JSON fixture matching the doGet output shape.
deployment_notes: GitHub Pages serves index.html as the entry point.
When the Apps Script deployment URL changes, FEED_URL in index.html must be updated and committed.
Any structural changes to voidfest-map-data.json or voidfest-map.svg IDs require coordinated updates in index.html.
If Bookwhen URLs remain sourced from void-space-inventory.csv, the JSON generation/update path must copy them into voidfest-map-data.json before deployment.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Copy Bookwhen URLs from void-space-inventory.csv into voidfest-map-data.json or teach the frontend to load them.
todo_and_open_issues.details: The frontend does not read the CSV. With empty bookwhen_url fields in JSON, published available shapes cannot open booking pages.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Make frontend publication filtering honor current data.
todo_and_open_issues.details: Either generate top-level published/unpublished arrays in voidfest-map-data.json or update isPublishedKey_ to inspect each shape's csv['Published Status']. Current fallback treats every shape as published.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Align Apps Script SLEEPER_KEYS with frontend/data sleeper rooms.
todo_and_open_issues.details: email_parser_script.gs is missing 239, 244, and 249 from SLEEPER_KEYS. This can break the privacy guarantee for those rooms.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Reconcile SDL capacity between email_parser_script.gs and index.html.
todo_and_open_issues.details: The parser default is SDL: 1 while frontend default is SDL: 2. The frontend often uses csv.capacity_1_to_4, but default mismatches are risky once live feed is enabled.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Resolve or explicitly document combined vendor spot 'ij'.
todo_and_open_issues.details: void-space-review.json reports one remaining review key, 'ij', described in inventory as combined I + J vendor spot.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Set FEED_URL when ready for live occupancy.
todo_and_open_issues.details: index.html currently disables occupancy by setting FEED_URL to ''. This is intentional per README current state, but must change for the live booking map.

todo_and_open_issues.summary: Dream Tent artist booth key normalization appears implemented.
todo_and_open_issues.details: deriveSpaceKeyForRow and candidateFeedKeys_ now normalize dt-style keys to numeric feed keys and try fallback variants. Keep this as a regression watch item when changing map/data generation.
